page: Microsoft Azure D160ads_v7 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Dadsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD processor with 160 dedicated virtual CPUs. It is equipped with 640.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

For local storage requirements, the D160ads_v7 includes 9449 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage. The instance does not include any graphics processing units, making it a CPU-only compute resource. Additionally, it does not provide any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The hardware profile offers high resource density, combining a large allocation of dedicated vCPUs with substantial local storage capacity. This configuration is designed for workloads requiring significant parallel processing and local data caching. Appropriate workloads include large-scale databases, enterprise applications, data warehousing, and general-purpose business-critical applications that benefit from high core counts and local storage without requiring GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D160ads_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 160 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor, paired with 640.0 GB of system memory, which provides a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with 9449 GB of local disk storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This high-density configuration offers a qualitative balance of compute power and local storage capacity. It is designed for resource-intensive workloads such as large-scale databases, enterprise applications, data warehousing, and parallel processing tasks that require substantial CPU and local storage resources without the need for graphics acceleration.
